stories.body: The United States and Iran signed a memorandum at the Palace of Versailles that imposes an immediate cease-fire across all fronts, commits both governments to 60 days of talks on a lasting agreement that could be extended, and tasks negotiators with reopening the Strait of Hormuz and ending hostilities in Lebanon, though officials cautioned that further high-level meetings in Switzerland may still be delayed and that implementing the pact could take longer than the provisional deadline.
stories.headline: US and Iran sign accord to halt fighting and pursue permanent deal within 60 days

stories.body: Moscow mayor Sergei Sobyanin said roughly 180 incoming drones were intercepted but several struck the Kapotnya oil refinery, sparking flames, suspending traffic on the ring road, damaging housing and commercial buildings, and forcing Sheremetyevo airport to evacuate passengers, while British maritime authorities warned of potential Russian revenge over the Royal Marines’ seizure of the tanker Smyrtos even as Kyiv endured further missile barrages and both sides traded accusations of targeting civilians.
stories.headline: Ukrainian drones ignite Moscow refinery as UK shipping braces for Russian retaliation

stories.body: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi told Lebanon’s Al Mayadeen that any broader pact with Washington must include an Israeli pullout from southern Lebanon and a cease-fire with Hezbollah, underscoring Tehran’s insistence that Lebanon remain integral to negotiations over ending the four-month war that has closed the Strait of Hormuz, while Iranian officials vowed continued support for Hezbollah amid ongoing cross-border strikes and U.N. warnings about hunger driven by disrupted fuel supplies.
stories.headline: Iran ties interim peace to Hezbollah front as it demands Israeli withdrawal from southern Lebanon

stories.body: NASA ordered five astronauts into their Crew Dragon safe haven for about two hours after air loss aboard Russia’s Zvezda service module doubled to two pounds per day, but reversed the directive once Roscosmos paused a disputed plan to saw through panels to reach a crack, highlighting ongoing U.S.–Russian disagreements over diagnosing and repairing leaks that have persisted for months yet have never forced an evacuation in the space station’s 27-year history.
stories.headline: NASA rescinds ISS evacuation after reassessing Russian module air leak

stories.body: The Bank of Japan raised its short-term policy rate by 25 basis points to 1%, citing faster pass-through of war-induced energy costs even as core inflation slipped to 1.4% in April, with Governor Shinichi Uchida calling the U.S.–Iran memorandum a welcome step but warning that underlying inflation risks require tighter policy, a move that pushed the Nikkei to a record 70,000 points and marked only the second G7 rate hike since the war began.
stories.headline: Bank of Japan lifts policy rate to 1% for first time since 1995 despite easing oil prices

stories.body: Prominent GOP figures including Senators Bill Cassidy, Thomas Massie and Nikki Haley blasted President Donald Trump’s 14-point memorandum with Iran, arguing it offers Tehran a $300 billion reconstruction plan without enforceable limits on its nuclear program, rewards threats to the Strait of Hormuz, and mirrors the Obama-era deal Trump once repudiated, while former Vice President Mike Pence warned the agreement amounts to appeasement unless Iran dismantles its missile and proxy networks.
stories.headline: Republicans lash Trump’s Iran memorandum over $300 billion reconstruction pledge

stories.body: Health care executive Rick Jackson poured more than $100 million of personal funds into Georgia’s Republican gubernatorial primary to defeat Lt. Gov. Burt Jones despite dual endorsements from President Trump and Gov. Brian Kemp, underscoring the power of self-funding in a marquee battleground as Jackson, a political newcomer who once donated to Liz Cheney, refashioned himself as a Trump ally and now faces Democrat Keisha Lance Bottoms in November.
stories.headline: Billionaire Rick Jackson beats Trump-backed Burt Jones in Georgia GOP governor runoff

stories.body: Rep. Mike Collins secured the Republican nomination to challenge Democratic Sen. Jon Ossoff after President Trump’s last-minute endorsement helped him defeat former coach Derek Dooley, ending a proxy battle between Trump and Gov. Brian Kemp and giving Collins momentum heading into a costly general election despite recent campaign turmoil involving a top aide’s vulgar social media post and an ongoing House Ethics probe.
stories.headline: Mike Collins wins Georgia GOP Senate runoff after late Trump endorsement

stories.body: In a 52-47 party-line vote the Senate approved an extra $70 billion for DHS immigration enforcement after rejecting Democratic efforts to kill President Trump’s $1.8 billion “anti-weaponization” fund, exposing GOP divisions as Senators Susan Collins, Jon Husted and Dan Sullivan briefly backed a procedural move against the fund before opposing final amendments, while Republicans argued tougher deportation resources are needed despite $100 billion in previously unspent DHS funds.
stories.headline: Senate passes $70 billion ICE funding bill while keeping Trump’s legal-defense fund intact

stories.body: With Washington, DC set to elect its first new mayor and delegate in over a decade, leading candidates Janeese Lewis George, Kenyan McDuffie, Robert White and Brooke Pinto are campaigning against President Trump’s federalization of local police, National Guard deployments and immigration raids, promising to sever MPD-ICE cooperation and build congressional coalitions to defend home rule even as the White House signals it could tighten control if opponents prevail.
stories.headline: DC elections set up clash with Trump over federal crackdowns and home rule

stories.body: Former Prime Minister José Luis Rodríguez Zapatero denied influencing the Plus Ultra airline bailout or receiving illicit commissions, but National Court Judge José Luis Calama ruled that his statements did not negate evidence of alleged influence peddling, money laundering, tax fraud and smuggling tied to offshore structures, bank transfers from public aid, and undeclared jewels worth more than €1.3 million that investigators say constitute unjustified wealth.
stories.headline: Judge keeps Plus Ultra corruption probe alive after Zapatero’s testimony fails to dispel evidence

stories.body: With corruption probes engulfing the PSOE and a parliamentary majority of PP, Vox, Junts, UPN and now the Basque Nationalist Party demanding new elections, PNV leaders warned Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez that their support hinges on him finally presenting a 2026 budget; if it fails, they say he should dissolve parliament, accusing the government of presiding over an agonizing legislature while Sánchez insists on serving through summer 2027.
stories.headline: PNV tells Sánchez to present a budget or call early elections as pressure mounts

stories.body: Spain’s Popular Party will file a constitutional appeal after the Congress board, dominated by PSOE and Sumar, blocked PP and Junts amendments urging Sánchez to call early elections, a move PP argues is arbitrary because the chamber has previously debated motions urging the prime minister to exercise exclusive powers, and because PP, Vox and Junts now command 177 seats that would likely approve the symbolic demand if allowed to vote.
stories.headline: PP to challenge congressional veto on election-demand motion at Constitutional Court

stories.body: Barcelona’s PSC-led city hall, backed by ERC and Barcelona en Comú, agreed to raise the municipal surcharge on cruise passengers staying less than 12 hours from €8 to €24—€30 once the regional levy is included—and to explore annual and monthly limits on cruise arrivals to keep traffic near 3.5 million passengers, though Junts, PP and Vox opposed both the higher tax and potential caps.
stories.headline: Barcelona moves to triple cruise visitor surcharge and study caps on arrivals

stories.body: Mayor Jaume Collboni announced that Barcelona will install 75 temporary shade canopies this summer across playgrounds, plazas and schoolyards as part of the Ombres program, aiming for more than 200 shaded spaces and 40,000 square meters of cover by 2027 with an €11 million budget, while bespoke installations for the seafront, Plaça Comercial and Portal de l’Àngel will follow in 2027-2028 to help protect residents from intensifying heat.
stories.headline: City to add 75 seasonal shade structures and keep over 500 cooling refuges open this summer

stories.body: Incumbent síndic David Bondia and challenger Gemma Calvet clashed on betevé over housing, homelessness and the institution’s visibility, with Calvet accusing Bondia of neglecting gender-based violence in annual reports and calling for a hybrid, participatory selection process, while Bondia defended outreach gains and presented documentation of work on violence against women as citizens vote in a nonbinding Decidim poll before the city council appointment.
stories.headline: Barcelona ombudsman race turns combative in debate between David Bondia and Gemma Calvet

stories.body: Following three fatal shootings tied to criminal feuds, the city council approved proposals from PSC, Junts, Barcelona en Comú and ERC urging Madrid to raise prison terms for illegal gun possession from two to five years and for large-scale marijuana cultivation from one to three years up to six to nine, alongside neighborhood security plans in la Marina and Poble-sec, although Comuns warned disproportionate cannabis penalties could push gangs toward harder drugs and emphasized intelligence-led policing.
stories.headline: Barcelona seeks tougher gun and cannabis penalties and targeted policing after deadly shootings
